title: Communauté d'agglomération Plaine Vallée
summary: Communauté d'agglomération Plaine Vallée is the communauté d'agglomération, an intercommunal structure, covering northwestern suburbs of Paris. It is located in the Val-d'Oise department, in the Île-de-France region, northern France. Established in November 2015, effective from January 2016, its seat is in Montmorency. Its area is 74.1 km,2 and its population was 184,945 in 2021.
text:
Communauté d'agglomération Plaine Vallée is the communauté d'agglomération, an intercommunal structure, covering northwestern suburbs of Paris. It is located in the Val-d'Oise department, in the Île-de-France region, northern France. Established in November 2015, effective from January 2016, its seat is in Montmorency. Its area is 74.1 km,2 and its population was 184,945 in 2021.

Composition
The communauté d'agglomération consists of the following 18 communes:
